abstract.text: This article describes the KeyboardKit autocomplete engine.

inlineContent.text: Autocomplete is an important part of the typing experience, where word suggestions can be presented in a toolbar above the keyboard as the user types, and incorrectly text can be autocorrected.
can provide suggestions and predictions to the main
. Unlike other services, you must use KeyboardKit Pro to unlock local autocomplete or implement it yourself.

support, unless you want to implement it from scratch. You can sign up for KeyboardKit Pro from the

performs on-device autocomplete by deafult. It works offline, doesn’t require Full Access, integrates with system services like the local lexicon, and supports the following languages:

The on-device tools that are provided by Apple are not as good as the ones in the native keyboards. Make sure to test this feature in the demo app to ensure that is is good enough for your needs, before you purchase a license.

AI-based next word prediction requires Full Access. It’s disabled by default, to avoid sending typed text to 3rd parties without the user’s explicit consent. When a user has given its explicit content, you can enable it with
